mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat during periods of CPI acceleration, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly increases corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no correlation with industrial production growth, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth dropping below 100,000 per month indicates labor market slack and weakening economic momentum, which reduces earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of months where payroll growth falls below the threshold, or the signal information coefficient turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable inflation indicates healthy demand expansion without overheating, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns when GDP growth exceeds 4% annually, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates equity returns only during periods of low interest rates, as cheap capital amplifies the valuation impact of rising manufacturing output over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces no alpha when federal funds rates exceed 3.0%, or forward returns decouple from production data during financial stress episodes.
